stories.headline: US and Israel launch joint military strikes on Iran, killing Supreme Leader Khamenei and sparking wider regional conflict

articles.title: Iranian drone hit RAF base, Cyprus president says
articles.description: No casualties were reported and the base sustained "minimal damage", the Ministry of Defence said.

articles.title: Oil prices jump and shares fall as conflict escalates
articles.description: Experts have warned that a prolonged conflict could push global energy prices even higher.

articles.title: Video appears to show US F-15 fighter jet crashing in Kuwait
articles.description: Kuwait's ministry of defence confirmed "several American fighter aircraft came down this morning", with crews "unscathed".

articles.title: Allies of US in the Gulf bear brunt of Iran attacks
articles.description: Iran's attacks on Gulf Arab states suggest the Islamic Republic is targeting not just the US military but also civilian infrastructure.

articles.title: Iran's regime is still intact - the coming days will show if it can hold out
articles.description: Iran's surviving leaders are in crisis mode, battling to project security as US and Israel strikes continue.

articles.title: UK will allow US to use bases to strike Iranian missile sites, PM says
articles.description: The prime minister says it remains the case that the UK will "not join offensive action now".

articles.title: Iran, the US, and a World Cup that starts in three months
articles.description: What does the US conflict with Iran mean for what was already set to be a highly politicised World Cup this summer?

articles.title: Hezbollah strikes Israel as American and Israeli planes pound Iran
articles.description: Iran and Iranian-backed militias fired missiles at Israel and Arab states, apparently hitting the U.S. Embassy compound in Kuwait, while Israel and the United States pounded targets in Iran on Monday.

articles.title: Congress gears up for vote on Trump's war powers in Iran — after the battle began
articles.description: Democrats and a few Republicans are calling for curbing Trump's unilateral use of military power in Iran, despite previous such efforts failing to advance.

articles.title: 7 key points in U.S.-Iran relations since 1953
articles.description: The U.S. and Iran have a long history of tensions, including a CIA-led campaign to topple Iran's prime minister in 1953 and the taking of American hostages in 1979.

articles.title: Prediction market trader 'Magamyman' made $553,000 on death of Iran's supreme leader
articles.description: It's the latest trade drawing scrutiny on the popular prediction market site for appearing to show an insider making profits on military secrets.

articles.title: Oil prices rise sharply in market trading after attacks in Middle East disrupt supply
articles.description: The high prices came as U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran and retaliatory strikes against Israel and U.S. military installations around the Gulf sent disruptions through the global energy supply chain.

articles.title: Iran live updates: Hegseth to hold Pentagon briefing Monday morning
articles.description: The U.S. and Israel launched a joint attack on Iran on Saturday, officials said.

articles.title: Department of Homeland Security warns of potential attacks amid Iran operation
articles.description: The Department of Homeland Security has warned of potential lone-wolf and cyberattacks amid the ongoing strikes in Iran, according to a law enforcement bulletin.

articles.title: Oil prices soar and stock prices fall as US-Israel war with Iran rattles markets
articles.description: The U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran have sent a chill through world markets, with U.S. futures falling more than 1%

articles.title: WATCH:  Protests, celebrations over US strikes on Iran
articles.description: In the U.S., Iranians and Iranian-Americans are taking to the streets to celebrate and protest the U.S. military strikes and the death of the Iranian supreme leader. ABC News' Jaclyn Lee reports.

articles.title: WATCH:  The latest as the US and Israel attack Iran
articles.description: The Middle East was rocked by another night of air attacks pitting the U.S. and Israel against Iran. ABC News' Chief Foreign Correspondent Ian Pannell reports from Tel Aviv, Israel.

articles.title: WATCH:  Americans scramble to leave Middle East amid strikes
articles.description: The fighting in the region is leaving thousands of Americans there uncertain about their situation. ABC News' Chief International Correspondent James Longman reports from Turkey.

articles.title: WATCH:  What strikes on Iran mean for the Middle East
articles.description: Former U.S. ambassador to NATO retired Lt. Gen. Doug Lute discusses the possible fallout from the joint U.S.-Israel attack.

articles.title: WATCH:  At least 201 people had been killed across Iran: Iranian Red Crescent Society
articles.description: Michael Singh, the managing director and senior fellow at the Washington Institute, joins to examine the U.S. military strikes in the Middle East.

articles.title: WATCH:  Missiles land in Dubai
articles.description: ABC News’ Lama Hasan reports from the region on the Iranian response to U.S. attacks, with the UAE saying a civilian was killed by falling debris.

articles.title: WATCH:  Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman says president, supreme leader 'safe and sound'

articles.title: Deaths, travel chaos mount as Iran lashes out at U.S. allies across Gulf

articles.title: Trump says Iran operation could take "four weeks or less," 3 U.S. troops killed
articles.description: The U.S. military says three troops have been killed in the war with Iran, as President Trump says the operation is proceeding "ahead of schedule." Follow live updates.

articles.title: 3/1: Face The Nation
articles.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," as the U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran continue into a second day after the death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Senate Intelligence Committee Chairman Tom Cotton joins, as well as Sens. Chris Murphy and Ted Cruz.

articles.title: 3/1: CBS Weekend News
articles.description: Another wave of U.S. strikes on Iran; Iran launches strikes across Gulf countries.

articles.title: 3/1/2026: Iran; Under Siege; Breaking the Cycle

articles.title: World leaders and governments react to U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran

articles.title: Americans stranded in the Middle East amid strikes

articles.title: Trump says operations will "continue until all of our objectives are achieved"
articles.description: "Sadly, there will likely be more before it ends. That's the way it is. Likely be more," President Trump said after mentioning the three U.S. service members killed in the operation.

articles.title: Who takes over in Iran with Khamenei dead?
articles.description: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed along with about 40 other senior members of the Islamic Republic regime in the U.S.-Israeli strikes on Saturday. Matt Gutman has more on the succession plan.

articles.title: As Trump Bombs Iran, America’s Allies Watch Fitfully From Sidelines

articles.title: Trump says more American casualties ‘likely’ during Iran operation

articles.title: How Trump decided to strike Iran

articles.title: Khamenei's death met with public mourning, quiet celebrations as regime's opponents wait for an opening

articles.title: Video shows U.S. warplane crashing in Kuwait
articles.description: Video shows the moment a U.S. warplane crashed near an American military base in Kuwait. The cause of the crash is under investigation, but Kuwaiti officials said all crew members survived.

articles.title: Attack on Iran threatens to explode into wider Middle East conflict
articles.description: As the U.S., Israel and Iran trade strikes in the Middle East, the conflict is threatening to explode into a much wider regional conflict. Iran is retaliating against U.S. allies like Kuwait, Bahrain, the United Arab Emirates and Jordan. NBC News’ Keir Simmons reports from Dubai.

articles.title: Khamenei's death met with public mourning, quiet celebrations as regime's opponents wait for an opening

articles.title: U.S., Israel and Iran trade new strikes as conflict escalates
articles.description: Three U.S. service members have been killed in action and five more have been seriously wounded in military operations against Iran, the U.S. military said Sunday. It comes as Israel launched a new wave of strikes in Tehran as it continued to hit targets in Iran a day after the country's supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, was killed. NBC News’ Richard Engel reports from Tel Aviv.

articles.title: Republicans praise U.S. strikes on Iran as Democrats question the administration's strategy
articles.description: Members of Congress split largely along party lines in their assessments of the U.S. and Israeli military strikes on Iran, with Republicans mostly backing the operation and Democrats urging the administration to seek congressional approval and questioning President Donald Trump's strategy.

articles.title: Victims of missile strike outside of Jerusalem were taking cover in bomb shelter

Stability and peace are not achieved through mutual threats, nor through…

articles.description: Democrat Doug Schoen defends Trump's Iran strike with Israel, criticizing his own party for condemning the action that killed Iran's supreme leader.

Kuwait’s Ministry of Defense released a statement about the U.S. jet crashes that was posted by […]

articles.description: The head of Hezbollah‘s parliamentary bloc in Lebanon’s government was killed during a military strike conducted by Israeli Defense Forces on Sunday. Mohammad Raad was among ten people killed by airstrikes in Beirut, Lebanon.  Raad was a member of the Lebanese Shia Islamist party and leader of the Loyalty to the Resistance Bloc, according to […]

articles.description: The national average price for regular gas rose to $2.984 per gallon on Sunday to begin March.  The new pricing marks a five-cent increase from a week ago and slightly over an 11-cent rise since the beginning of February, when gas was $2.87 per gallon. With the current military conflict between Iran and the United […]

articles.description: Israel launched targeted strikes on Hezbollah, an Iranian terrorist proxy, in Lebanon, its military announced on Sunday night. The Israeli military struck “senior” Hezbollah terrorists in Beirut and one “central” Hezbollah terrorist in southern Lebanon, according to its air force. It also urged civilians in nearly 50 villages in Lebanon to evacuate ahead of what […]

articles.description: President Donald Trump did not speak to reporters after returning to Washington, D.C., on Sunday night, dodging questions about Iran and instead admiring new statues in the Rose Garden of the White House. The U.S. military began a large-scale military operation against Iran over the weekend with Israel, battering the regime’s military infrastructure and killing […]

In a joint statement on Sunday, leaders of the United Kingdom, France, and Germany said they are “appalled” by Iran’s attacks on Gulf countries, which were launched in retaliation for […]

Likely be more.” His comments follow U.S. Central Command’s announcement that three U.S. soldiers have […]

articles.title: In Iran, Trump Reaches a Turning Point

analysis.summary: The US and Israel launched joint military strikes on Iran beginning Saturday, February 28, 2026, in an operation dubbed 'Operation Epic Fury,' killing Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and approximately 40 senior regime officials. Iran retaliated with missile and drone strikes against Israel and US allies across the Gulf region, killing at least 3 US service members, while the conflict spread to include Hezbollah exchanges with Israel in Lebanon, attacks on Gulf states including Kuwait, Bahrain, the UAE, and Qatar, and a drone strike on a British base in Cyprus. Oil prices surged sharply, thousands of flights were canceled, and global markets fell as fears of a prolonged wider regional conflict intensified.
agreements: The US and Israel launched joint military strikes on Iran starting Saturday, February 28, 2026
Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in the strikes along with dozens of senior regime officials
Three US service members were killed in action and five were seriously wounded
Iran launched retaliatory strikes against Israel and multiple US-allied Gulf states including Kuwait, Bahrain, the UAE, and Qatar
Oil prices rose sharply following the strikes, with Brent crude up approximately 9 percent
US fighter jets crashed in Kuwait with all crew members surviving
Trump acknowledged more American casualties were likely and said the operation could last four weeks or less
The UK authorized US use of British bases for limited strikes on Iranian missile capabilities while declining to join offensive operations
More than 2,400 flights were canceled across the Middle East due to airspace closures
Thousands of Americans were stranded in the Middle East
Israel closed Gaza border crossings amid the strikes
Israel launched strikes on Hezbollah targets in Lebanon as the conflict widened
At least nine people were killed in a missile strike on Beit Shemesh near Jerusalem
Democrats pushed for a war powers vote while Republicans largely supported the operation
Trump called the operation 'Operation Epic Fury' and said it would continue until all objectives were achieved
At least 201 people were reported killed across Iran according to the Iranian Red Crescent Society

disagreements.topic: Characterization of Khamenei's death

positions.position: Consistently refers to the killing as an 'assassination,' framing it as a targeted political killing that 'will likely backfire'
Celebrates it as deserved justice, writing 'the despot met his fate at the hands of the nations he devoted his life to destroying'
Frames it through a pop-culture lens as Trump winning 'his ultimate FAFO feud' with Khamenei
Uses neutral language, reporting Iran's 'surviving leaders are in crisis mode' without characterizing the killing as justified or unjustified

disagreements.topic: Whether the strikes and regime change strategy are wise or justified

positions.position: Strongly supports the strikes and explicitly argues 'the time for regime change in Iran has arrived' and that only regime change will end the threat
Publishes opinion pieces supporting the strikes, with a Democrat columnist saying 'my party is wrong' to criticize them, and calling on Europe to back America
Frames the assassination as likely to backfire based on historical precedent, emphasizing long-term disaster over short-term gains
Reports that only 27% of Americans support the operation according to a Reuters/Ipsos poll, suggesting broad public opposition
Reports that many of Trump's own voters did not want to attack Iran, framing it as a political risk for the president

disagreements.topic: Congressional authority and legality of the strikes

positions.position: Frames the war powers debate as Congress trying to curb Trump's 'unilateral use of military power' after the fact, noting previous efforts failed
Reports a bipartisan war powers resolution will get a Senate vote, framing it as a genuine GOP-Trump confrontation with Democrats
Reports the split 'largely along party lines' with Republicans backing the operation and Democrats urging congressional approval
Does not prominently feature the war powers debate in its coverage, focusing instead on opinion pieces supporting the strikes

disagreements.topic: Iran's targeting strategy in its retaliatory strikes

positions.position: Reports Iran is 'targeting not just the US military but also civilian infrastructure' in Gulf Arab states
Describes the strikes as 'retaliatory' attacks on 'US assets in the region,' framing them as responses to aggression
Frames Iran's retaliation as attacks on US allies, prompting European powers to 'jump into the fray' to defend Gulf allies

disagreements.topic: Whether Iran's initial claims about leadership survival were credible

positions.position: Aired an interview with Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman who claimed the president and supreme leader were 'safe and sound' after the initial strikes
Reported Khamenei was confirmed killed along with about 40 senior regime members, treating his death as established fact by Day 2

disagreements.topic: Role and responsibility of European allies

positions.position: Frames European leaders as sidelined bystanders 'disregarded by President Trump,' adapting to irrelevance
Former US ambassador criticizes Brussels' 'weak response' and demands Europe 'stand with America'
Reports UK, France, and Germany are actively stepping up, vowing to defend Gulf allies and setting up increased defensive postures
Focuses on UK's specific position: allowing base use but not joining offensive action

disagreements.topic: Framing of domestic opposition to the strikes

positions.position: Highlights both protests outside the White House and the mixed emotions of the Iranian diaspora, including 'joy, unease, frustration, hope, dread'
Reports both protests and celebrations among Iranian Americans, presenting a balanced domestic reaction
Criticizes American media for giving 'malign regimes a pass' as long as they are perceived as enemies of Trump
Reports Marjorie Taylor Greene, a Republican, called the US service member deaths 'unnecessary and unacceptable,' highlighting intra-party dissent

uniqueAngles.angle: Detailed reporting on a Polymarket prediction market trader called 'Magamyman' who made $553,000 betting on Khamenei's death, raising insider trading concerns tied to military secrets
Provided historical context with '7 key points in U.S.-Iran relations since 1953,' including the CIA-led coup and the 1979 hostage crisis
Reported on the humanitarian impact in Gaza, where crossing closures sparked panic buying and fears of food shortages among residents
Reported that Iran's Ali Larijani refuted US media claims that Tehran pushed to resume talks, offering a direct Iranian government counter-narrative
Reported on missile debris sparking a fire at Saudi Arabia's Aramco oil refinery, a detail not prominently covered by US outlets
Explored how the conflict could affect the upcoming FIFA World Cup starting in three months, given both the US and Iran are participating
Reported that an Iranian drone hit an RAF base in Cyprus, with the Cyprus president confirming; detailed as the first strike on a British base in Cyprus since 1986
Featured an interview with Reza Pahlavi, the exiled Iranian crown prince, on 60 Minutes discussing the future of Iran
Provided detailed financial analysis including JPMorgan's path to $120/barrel oil, gasoil spiking more than crude due to the Saudi refinery strike, and the critical Strait of Hormuz chokepoint
Reported on broader prediction market controversies including both Polymarket and Kalshi, detailing a firestorm over handling of sensitive military-related markets
Analyzed how the Iran strikes could reshape Democratic primary election dynamics, with AIPAC money and intervention stances becoming new litmus tests
Reported that many of Trump's own voters were not enthusiastic about attacking Iran even before the strikes, framing it as a coalition management challenge

Reported Marjorie Taylor Greene's criticism of the operation as 'absolutely unnecessary,' notable as rare Republican opposition

Detailed the diplomatic timeline of how Trump decided to strike, including a last-chance negotiation in Geneva on Thursday where US negotiators told Iran not to take steps toward a nuclear bomb
Provided on-the-ground reporting from Beit Shemesh showing victims were killed while sheltering in a bomb shelter when missiles struck

Tracked granular gas price data, noting prices rose to $2.984 per gallon, up five cents from a week prior and eleven cents since February began

Reported on Trump giving 'competing visions of new regime' in Iran, including suggesting Iran's military should 'simply surrender their weapons to the Iranian public'

stories.headline: Texas Senate primary races heat up with contentious Democratic and Republican contests ahead of Tuesday vote

James Talarico (D) and state Attorney General Ken Paxton (R) have single-digit leads in their respective Texas Senate primary races. The survey, released Sunday, found that 52 percent of likely Democratic primary voters backed Talarico, while 47 percent backed Rep. Jasmine Crockett (D). Paxton,…

analysis.summary: Texas Senate primaries on Tuesday feature contentious races on both sides. The Democratic primary pits U.S. Rep. Jasmine Crockett against state Rep. James Talarico in a bitter contest laden with racial overtones and electability debates, while the Republican primary features Attorney General Ken Paxton against allies of the party establishment, raising concerns about the general election. Polls show slim leads for Talarico and Paxton heading into Tuesday's vote.

The case has drawn attention from both conservative gun rights groups and liberal civil liberties organizations.

articles.title: Will Democrats Be Able to Force Disclosure of Jack Smith’s Mar-a-Lago Report?
articles.description: If they take control of a chamber of Congress next year, all bets are off.

articles.title: Judge Cannon’s Flawed Order to Suppress Jack Smith’s Report on the Mar-a-Lago Documents Caper
articles.description: Federal courts do not actually have sweeping ‘supervisory authority’ over the DOJ.
